original_abstract: As scientific literature and technology evolve at an unprecedented pace, scholars are increasingly turning to advanced artificial intelligence models to streamline research and improve information accessibility. The emergence of OpenAI’s ChatGPT represents a transformative milestone in large language model (LLM) technology, fundamentally altering the landscape of AI. Since its debut, various sectors have explored ChatGPT’s utility, focusing on its sophisticated natural language processing and conversational capabilities. This article investigates the critical intersection of generative AI and governance, asking to what extent ChatGPT can influence public policy analysis. The researcher evaluates the model’s potential to enhance data-driven decision-making while addressing the persistent challenges—such as algorithmic transparency, inherent bias, and contextual limitations—that researchers and policymakers must navigate.
humanized_abstract: The pace of research in the scientific literature and the technologies involved are advancing at a rapid rate, in which researchers are increasingly dependent on new artificial intelligence technologies in order to accelerate their work and gain access to the information they need in a faster manner. OpenAI’s ChatGPT is a new large language model (LLM) that has revolutionised the way these new models function and thus, it is being tested in a plethora of different fields in which it is believed that it may offer a breakthrough via its language processing and dialogue functions. This post will look at the governance of generative AI, especially in relation to the effect ChatGPT can have on the process of public policy analysis. In essence, the study is investigating the potential of ChatGPT and other models to contribute to the more datadriven policy-making practice, while at the same time identifying and tackling the range of challenges – among them explainability, the presence of bias, and contextual limitations – that researchers and policy-makers currently meet in their work.
